steps.title: Find the drip rate
steps.content: The faucet adds $0.06$ milliliter every $3$ seconds. Convert that to milliliters per second by dividing the drop volume by the number of seconds per drop.

steps.title: Compute the unit rate
steps.content:
The rate is
$$\frac{0.06}{3}=0.02$$
So the faucet adds $0.02$ milliliter each second. After $t$ seconds, the added volume is $0.02t$.

steps.title: Include the starting volume
steps.content: The container already has $18$ milliliters of water before any new water drips in. Add that initial amount to the volume gained from the faucet.

steps.title: Write the volume model
steps.content: Combining the initial volume and the added volume gives $$v=0.02t+18$$ The answer is A.
